The watercraft has no brakes; do not release the throttle when trying to steer away from objects because you need throttle to steer.",["Manual20_44", "Manual20_46", "Manual20_48", "Manual20_88", "Manual20_89", "jetski_06"]

"Practice reboarding in shallow water before riding in deep water. For boarding with a passenger, do not exceed the total load limit of 160 kg (353 lb), including cargo, and do not apply throttle until the passenger is seated with both feet on the footwell floor and holding on to the operator.
1. The passenger steadies the watercraft while the operator boards from the rear platform, pulls up into a seated position, and balances the watercraft.<PIC>
2. Attach the lanyard to your left wrist, install its clip on the engine shut-off switch, start the engine, and keep it at idle.
3. The passenger pulls up onto the platform, kneels, then crawls onto the seat as the watercraft accelerates.<PIC>
4. Once both riders are seated, accelerate to planing speed, then reduce throttle to the desired running speed.<PIC>
Before starting off, look in all directions and make sure there are no boats, obstructions, or swimmers around you.",["jetski_06", "jetski_07", "jetski_08"]

"Do not operate the watercraft if the total weight of operator, passenger, and cargo exceeds 160 kg (353 lb). If it is difficult to balance at a standstill, use this boarding sequence:
1. Have the passenger steady the watercraft while the operator boards; the operator pulls up onto the platform into a seated position, then balances the watercraft.<PIC>
2. Attach the lanyard to your left wrist, install the clip on the lanyard to the engine shut-off switch, start the engine, and keep it at idle.
3. Have the passenger pull up onto the platform, kneel down, and crawl onto the seat as the watercraft accelerates.<PIC>
4. Once both riders are seated and stable, accelerate to planing speed, then reduce the throttle to the desired running speed.<PIC>",["jetski_06", "jetski_07", "jetski_08"]

"The maximum load is 160 kg (353 lb), counted as the total weight of cargo, operator, and passenger. The WaveRunner is designed for the operator and one passenger only, with no more than 2 people aboard.",[]

"The Philips XL490 base station has one main base button: press it to find handsets, or press and hold it to enter registration mode.<PIC>\nThe Philips XL495 base station has the same find-handsets and registration-mode button, and its base also includes a speaker.<PIC>",["Manual22_18", "Manual22_19"]

"1. Check that the power supply voltage matches the voltage printed on the back or underside of the phone, and use only the supplied power adapter.
2. Connect the power adapter between the DC input jack on the bottom of the base station and the wall power socket.<PIC>
3. Connect the line cord between the telephone socket on the bottom of the base station and the wall telephone socket.
4. If your telephone line also carries DSL high-speed internet service, install a DSL filter between the telephone line cord and the wall socket to prevent noise and caller ID problems.",["Manual22_22"]

"1. The handset batteries are pre-installed. Pull the battery tape out from the battery door before charging, as shown by the tab being removed from the handset.<PIC>\n2. Place the handset on the base station to charge it. When it is placed correctly, you hear a docking sound and the handset starts charging.\n3. Charge the batteries for 8 hours before first use. Use only the supplied batteries, keep batteries away from heat, sunshine, and fire, and check polarity if you ever insert batteries in the compartment.",["Manual22_23"]

"Check the handset display. The battery icon on the screen shows the current battery level.<PIC>\nWhen the handset is off the base station or charger, the bars in the battery icon show the level from full to low. When the handset is on the base station or charger, the bars keep scrolling until charging is complete. If the empty battery icon flashes and you hear an alert tone, the battery is low and needs recharging.<PIC>",["Manual22_28", "Manual22_30"]

"1. Make sure the base station has power.\n2. Register the handset to the base station. If you need to do it manually, select [Menu] > [Services] > [Register], press [OK], put the base station into registration mode for 5 seconds, enter the system PIN, then press [OK]. Registration should complete in less than 2 minutes.\n3. Move the handset closer to the base station.",[]

"Use the handset LED indicator as follows: On means audio boost is on; Off means the phone is in a call or standby mode; Flashing means there is a new message, a new voicemail, a missed call, or the phone is muted. For charging status, On means the handset is on the base or charging cradle in standby mode, or audio boost is on during a call; Off means the handset is off the base or charging cradle; Flashing means the phone is muted.<PIC>\nOn the XL495 base station, Steady on means there is no new message and the memory is not full, the handset is switched on, or the phone is in paging mode. Flashing means there is a new message and the memory is not full, an incoming call, recording an incoming message or announcement, message playback, or remote access/listening to messages from the handset. Fast flashing means there is no new message and the memory is full, or the base is in registration mode.<PIC>",["Manual22_40", "Manual22_46"]

"For the XL495 base station, read the LED as a message/status indicator: steady on means there is no new message and the memory is not full, the answering machine is switched on, or paging mode is being entered. Flashing means there is a new message and memory is not full, an incoming call, recording of an incoming message or announcement, message playback, or remote access/listening to messages from the handset. Fast flashing means there is no new message and the memory is full, or registration mode is being entered.<PIC>",["Manual22_46"]

"Lower the roll bar only when absolutely necessary; there is no rollover protection when it is down, so do not wear the seat belt in the down position, drive slowly and carefully, and raise the roll bar again as soon as clearance permits.
1. Remove the hairpin cotters and the 2 pins from the roll bar pivot brackets; the pins and hairpin cotters are the retainers shown at the side brackets.<PIC>
2. Lower the roll bar to one of the two down positions shown for the machine.<PIC>
3. Reinstall the 2 pins and secure them with the hairpin cotters.",["Manual23_33", "Manual23_32"]

"On machines with the suspension system, adjust the two rear-shock assemblies to set ride comfort. The slot guide marks position 1 as firmest, position 2 as softest, and the detents as reference points only; the assemblies can be set anywhere in the slots.<PIC>
1. Unlock the rear-shock assembly latch.
2. Slide the assembly in its slot to the desired ride position.
3. Adjust the left and right rear-shock assemblies to the same position.
4. Lock the latch after setting the position; the adjustment graphic shows the correct matched and locked arrangement.<PIC>",["Manual23_37", "Manual23_38"]

"1. Push up on the deck-lift switch to raise the mower deck; push down on the same switch to lower it.<PIC>\n2. Choose the hole in the height-of-cut bracket that matches the desired cutting height, then insert the height-of-cut pin in that hole.<PIC>\n3. Whenever you change the height of cut, adjust the anti-scalp rollers.",["Manual23_55", "Manual23_56"]

"Use a heavy-duty trailer or truck with a full-width ramp. The ramp must not exceed a 15-degree angle, and its length should be at least 4 times the height from the trailer or truck bed to the ground.<PIC>
1. Connect the trailer to the towing vehicle and connect the safety chains.
2. If applicable, connect the trailer brakes and lights.
3. Lower the ramp and confirm the ramp-to-ground angle is no more than 15 degrees.
4. Back the machine up the ramp; when unloading, drive it forward down the ramp.<PIC>
5. Shut off the engine, remove the key, and engage the parking brake.
6. Tie down the machine near the front caster wheels and the rear bumper with straps, chains, cable, or ropes at the tie-down loops.<PIC>
Use extreme caution on the ramp, do not use individual ramps for each side of the mower, and avoid sudden acceleration or deceleration because loading can cause tip-over, serious injury, or death.",["Manual23_63", "Manual23_64", "Manual23_65"]

"Unload it with a full-width ramp, not separate individual ramps. Set the ramp so the angle between the ramp and the ground does not exceed 15 degrees; the manual illustrates this with a ramp at least 4 times as long as the trailer or truck-bed height from the ground.<PIC>
1. Lower the ramp and confirm the angle is 15 degrees or less.
2. Drive the machine forward down the ramp; the unloading direction shown is forward down the ramp, not backing down.<PIC>
Avoid sudden acceleration or deceleration while on the ramp because it can cause loss of control or tip-over.",["Manual23_63", "Manual23_64"]

"To change the Toro mower's engine oil:
1. Start the engine and let it run for 5 minutes so the oil drains better.
2. Park the machine with the drain side slightly lower than the opposite side, disengage the blade-control switch (PTO), engage the parking brake, shut off the engine, remove the key, and wait for all moving parts to stop.
3. Place a drain pan under the oil-drain hose, pull the hose out, remove the drain plug, drain the oil, then reinstall and tighten the plug and return the hose to its stored position.<PIC>
4. Slowly pour about 80% of the specified oil into the filler tube, then add oil gradually until the dipstick reads Full; do not overfill or underfill the crankcase.<PIC>
5. Start the engine, drive to a flat area, and check the oil level again. Dispose of used oil at a recycling center.
Use detergent engine oil. For Kawasaki engines use API SF, SG, SH, SJ, or SL; capacity is 2.3 L with filter/2.1 L without filter for FX751 and FX801, or 1.9 L with filter/1.7 L without filter for FX921. For Kohler engines use API SJ or higher; capacity is 1.9 L with filter or 1.6 L without filter.",["Manual23_75", "Manual23_76"]

"For the Toro Z Master 4000 air-cleaner filters, first park on a level surface, disengage the blade-control switch (PTO), engage the parking brake, shut off the engine, remove the key, and wait for all moving parts to stop.

If the mower has a Kawasaki engine: release the air-cleaner latches, pull the air-cleaner cover off the body, clean the inside of the cover with compressed air, gently slide the primary filter out without knocking it against the body, and remove the safety filter only if you are replacing it. The Kawasaki layout shows the air-cleaner body, primary filter, latches, cover, and safety filter.<PIC>

If the mower has a Kohler engine: release the air-cleaner latches, remove the air-inlet cover, clean the air-inlet screen and cover, reinstall and latch the air-inlet cover, then release the air-cleaner cover latches and pull the cover off the body. Clean the inside of the cover with compressed air, gently slide the primary filter out without knocking it against the body, and remove the inner filter only if you intend to replace it. The Kohler layout shows the inner filter, primary filter, air-cleaner cover, latch, and air-cleaner body.<PIC>",["Manual23_72", "Manual23_82"]

"1. Park the machine on a level surface, disengage the blade-control switch (PTO), and engage the parking brake.
2. Shut off the engine, remove the key, and wait for all moving parts to stop before leaving the operating position.
3. Lower the mower to the 76 mm (3 inches) height of cut.
4. Remove the belt covers.
5. Put a 3/8-inch ratchet in the square hole of the idler arm to relieve tension on the idler spring; use the belt-routing figure to identify the clutch pulley, mower belt, spring-loaded idler assembly, ratchet, and square hole in the idler arm.<PIC>
6. Remove the belt from the mower-deck pulleys and the clutch pulley, remove the belt guide on the spring-loaded idler arm, and remove the existing belt.
7. Install the new belt around the mower pulleys and the clutch pulley under the engine, then reinstall the belt guide on the idler arm.
8. Using the 3/8-inch ratchet in the square hole, install the idler spring. Ensure that the spring ends are seated in the anchor grooves.
9. Install the belt covers.",["Manual23_100"]

"Use the Control Set-Up pad, then select the setting number and the result number shown in the setup chart. The available controls are sound on/off, clock on/off, scroll speed, and LBS/KG defrost-weight choice.<PIC>
1. Touch Control Set-Up.
2. Touch the number for the function you want: 1 for sound, 2 for clock, 3 for scroll speed, or 4 for LBS/KG.
3. Touch the number for the result you want. For example, to change defrost weight from Lbs to Kg, touch Control Set-Up, touch 4, then touch 2.<PIC>",["Manual24_9", "Manual24_10"]

"Set the Light Timer to have the Lo Light turn on at the low setting and turn off automatically at the same times every day until you reset it.
1. Touch Light Timer once for AM or twice for PM to set the light-on time.
2. Enter the desired on time with the number pads.
3. Touch Light Timer again once for AM or twice for PM to set the light-off time.
4. Enter the desired off time with the number pads.
5. Touch Start. The manual's example shows 2:00 AM on and 7:00 AM off using Light Timer, 2 0 0, Light Timer again, 7 0 0, then Start.<PIC>
To change the on/off times, repeat the setting steps. To cancel the Light Timer while it is operating, touch Light HI/LO/Off.",["Manual24_11"]

"To set a Favorite Recipe, touch the Favorite Recipe pad, enter the cook time with the number keypad, then touch Start. For example, to program 2 minutes, press Favorite Recipe, then 2, 0, 0, then Start; the display counts down when cooking begins.<PIC>\nThe saved recipe uses Hi power by default, but the power level can be modified.",["Manual24_16"]

"Use the Sensor Reheat feature; it reheats without entering a cooking time or power level.
1. Put food at normal storage temperature in a microwavable container. Cover it loosely with a lid, waxed paper, or vented microwave-safe plastic wrap; do not use a tight-sealing plastic cover. Make sure the glass turntable, the outside of the container, and the oven interior are dry.
2. Touch Reheat, then select the food category: 1 for Casserole, 2 for Dinner Plate, or 3 for Soup/Sauce.<PIC>
3. For a casserole, the illustrated sequence is Reheat, number 1, then the oven displays SENSING while it measures the food.<PIC>
4. Stay within the recommended amounts: Casserole 1-4 cups, Dinner Plate 1-2 servings, Soup/Sauce 1-4 cups.<PIC>
5. Do not open the door or touch Stop/Clear during sensing. After sensing ends, the oven beeps twice and shows the remaining cook time; then you can stir, turn, or rearrange food if needed. At the end, it beeps four times and END displays.",["Manual24_29", "Manual24_30", "Manual24_31"]

"Use Auto Defrost for raw frozen meat, poultry, or fish; use Time Defrost for most other frozen foods.
1. Touch Auto Defrost.
2. Choose the category: 1 for meat, 2 for poultry, or 3 for fish.<PIC>
3. Enter the net food weight, excluding the container, in pounds and tenths of a pound. If the package shows fractions or ounces, convert it to a decimal pound value.
4. Touch Start. The display changes to a defrost time countdown and DEFROST appears; this is the basic sequence shown for a 1.2 lb ground beef example.<PIC>
5. When the oven beeps during the cycle, open the door, turn, separate, or rearrange the food, remove thawed portions, return frozen portions to the oven, and touch Start again.
Use Auto Defrost only for raw food. It works best from food at 0 degrees F directly from a freezer; enter a lower weight if the food came from a refrigerator-freezer that may be warmer than 5 degrees F or has been outside the freezer up to 20 minutes. Food must be 0.1-6.0 lb (0.1-4.0 kg), not more than 6.0 lb (4.0 kg). Keep any foil shields from touching the oven sides.",["Manual24_32", "Manual24_33"]

"Remove and clean the grease filter often, at least once a month.
1. Slide the filter to the side, pull it downward, then push it to the other side so it drops out from the hood bottom.<PIC>
2. Wash the filter in a dishwasher, or soak it in hot water with a mild detergent. Rinse well and shake it dry; do not use ammonia. The aluminum filter may darken in a dishwasher.<PIC>
3. Reinstall it by sliding it into the side slot, then pushing it up and toward the oven center until it locks.<PIC>
Do not operate the oven hood without the filter in place.",["Manual24_47", "Manual24_48", "Manual24_49"]

"If the oven is vented inside, replace the charcoal filter every 6 to 12 months, or more often if needed; it cannot be cleaned.
1. Unplug the oven or turn off power at the main power supply.
2. Remove the two middle vent-grill mounting screws.<PIC>
3. Tip the grill forward, lift it out, and remove the old charcoal filter from behind the grill opening.<PIC>
4. Slide the new charcoal filter into place so it rests at the shown angle.<PIC>
5. Slide the bottom of the grill into place, push the top until it snaps into place, replace the mounting screws, turn power back on at the main power supply, and set the clock.",["Manual24_50", "Manual24_51", "Manual24_52"]

"1. Unplug the microwave or turn off power at the main power supply. Wear gloves when replacing the light bulb.
2. Remove the two middle vent cover mounting screws, then tip the vent cover forward and lift it out to remove it.<PIC>
3. Remove the bulb holder, then lift the bulb holder up to access the oven light.<PIC>
4. Replace the bulb with a 30 or 40 watt appliance bulb.<PIC>
5. Reinstall the bulb holder. Slide the bottom of the vent cover into place, push the top until it snaps into place, replace the mounting screws, then turn the power back on or plug the oven in.",["Manual24_54", "Manual24_55", "Manual24_56"]

"Set the PCI Express 3.0 x16 slots by matching the slot use to the number of VGA/PCIe cards: with one card, install it in PCIe 3.0 x16_1 (gray), where it runs at x16 and is recommended for single VGA use; with two cards, use PCIe 3.0 x16_1 and PCIe 3.0 x16_2, both at x8; with three cards, use PCIe 3.0 x16_1 at x8, PCIe 3.0 x16_2 at x8, and PCIe 3.0 x16_3 at x4.<PIC> Provide sufficient power for some modes, and connect a chassis fan to CHA_FAN1/2/3 when using multiple graphics cards for better cooling. If you need to configure PCIe link speed in BIOS, use PCI Express Configuration > PCIe Speed, with options Auto, Gen1, Gen2, and Gen3.",["Manual25_31"]

"1. Turn OFF the computer and unplug the power cord before changing jumper settings.
2. To clear RTC RAM on the 2-pin CLRTC header, short the two pins with a metal object such as a screwdriver, plug the power cord back in, turn the computer on, then hold down the <Del> key during boot to enter BIOS Setup and reenter data. The CLRTC illustration identifies the two-pin header, with +3V_BAT and GND pins, on the motherboard.<PIC>
3. To set the 3-pin CPU Over Voltage jumper (CPU_OV), place the jumper cap on pins 2-3 to enable higher CPU voltage, or on pins 1-2 to return to the default CPU voltage setting. The CPU_OV illustration labels pins 1-2 as Disable/default and pins 2-3 as Enable.<PIC>
If clearing RTC RAM does not work, remove the onboard battery and short the CLRTC pins again, then reinstall the battery. If the system hangs because of overclocking, shut down and reboot so CPU Parameter Recall resets parameter settings automatically; you do not need to clear RTC.",["Manual25_33", "Manual25_34"]

"Set up the rear panel by matching each cable to the numbered port in the layout: 1 PS/2 mouse/keyboard combo, 2 DisplayPort, 3 VGA, 4 LAN RJ-45 Gigabit Ethernet, 5 orange center/subwoofer, 6 black rear speaker out, 7 light-blue line in, 8 lime line out or front speaker out in 4.1/5.1/7.1-channel audio, 9 pink microphone, 10 optical S/PDIF out, 11 USB 3.1 Type-A, 12 USB 3.1 Type-C, 13 USB 3.0 ports 3-6, 14 DVI-D, 15 HDMI, and 16 USB 2.0 ports 13 and 14.<PIC>\nUse USB 3.0 ports for USB 3.0 devices for better performance. USB 3.0 devices are for data storage, and the USB 2.0/3.0 rear ports are controlled by the xHCI controller, so some legacy USB devices may need firmware updates. For displays, multi-VGA output supports up to three displays in the operating-system environment, two in BIOS, and one in DOS; DVI-D is not compatible with DVI-I and cannot be converted to RGB/CRT.",["Manual25_35"]

"Set the configurable onboard lighting either in BIOS or with the LED Control app in Ai Suite 3.
1. For the model name LEDs, open BIOS and go to Advanced > Onboard Devices Configuration > Model Name LED Lighting. Choose Disabled, Still Mode, or Breathing Mode.
2. For the SupremeFX LED, go to Advanced > Onboard Devices Configuration > SupremeFX LED Lighting. Choose Disabled, Still Mode, Breathing Mode, or Flowing Mode.
The standby power LED is not a lighting-mode setting; it lights to indicate the system is on, in sleep mode, or in soft-off mode.",[]

"You can update the motherboard BIOS by using EZ Update in AI Suite 3, EZ Flash 3 in BIOS Setup, or BIOS Updater in a DOS environment. The manual's main in-BIOS method is EZ Flash 3:
1. Save a copy of the original BIOS file to a USB flash disk before updating.
2. Download the latest BIOS file for this motherboard. For a USB update, use a USB flash disk with a single FAT32/16 partition.
3. Enter BIOS Setup Advanced Mode, open the Tool menu, select EZ Flash 3 Utility, and press <Enter>.
4. Choose by USB or by Internet. For USB, insert the USB flash disk, select by USB, use <Tab> to move between the Drive and Folder Info fields, select the USB drive and BIOS file with the arrow keys, then press <Enter> to start the update. For Internet, select by Internet, choose the connection method with the left/right arrow keys, press <Enter>, and follow the onscreen instructions.
5. Reboot when the update finishes. Do not shut down or reset the system while the BIOS is updating, because this can cause boot failure.

To update in DOS instead, prepare the support DVD and a FAT32/16 single-partition USB flash drive containing the latest BIOS file and BIOS Updater, boot to FreeDOS, switch to the USB drive, run bupdater /pc /g, select the BIOS file, confirm Yes, exit after the update, restart, and load optimized defaults for stability.",[]

"1. Connect the hard drives to the Intel SATA connectors and make sure the drives have no existing RAID volumes.
2. Enter the BIOS and press <F11>, or click the EZ Tuning Wizard from the BIOS screen.
3. Click RAID, then click Yes to enable RAID.
4. When the available hard drives are displayed, click Next.
5. Select the storage type: Easy Backup or Super Speed, then click Next. For Easy Backup, choose Easy Backup (RAID1) or Easy Backup (RAID10); RAID10 is available only when four hard drives are connected. For Super Speed, choose Super Speed (RAID0) or Super Speed (RAID5), then click Next.
6. After selecting the RAID type, click Yes to continue setup. When setup finishes, click Yes to exit, then click OK to reset the system.",[]

"To install OS 7 with a SATA ODD and USB device on the 100 Series platform, use the support DVD to preload USB 3.0 drivers, then install from the USB OS source.
1. Prepare 1 support DVD, 1 OS 7 installation source, 1 SATA ODD, and 1 USB device, either a USB ODD or USB storage device. If using USB storage, it must be 8 GB or larger and should be formatted before use.
2. Put the OS 7 installation DVD in a USB ODD, or on a working system copy all files from the OS 7 installation DVD to the USB storage device.
3. Connect the USB ODD or USB storage device to the 100 Series platform.
4. Insert the support DVD into the SATA ODD on the 100 Series platform.
5. Power on the system and press F8 during POST to open the boot screen, then choose the USB ODD or USB storage device as the boot device; the boot screen lists available USB/DVD boot choices.<PIC>
6. During installation startup, the USB 3.0 driver loads automatically. When the driver has loaded correctly, the screen shows "Setup is starting...".<PIC>
7. Follow the onscreen instructions to complete the OS 7 installation.",["Manual25_87", "Manual25_88"]

"Place the motherboard in the chassis with the external-port edge toward the rear, then install nine screws in the circled screw holes to secure it.<PIC>\nDo not overtighten the screws, because this can damage the motherboard.",["Manual25_6"]

"1. Turn off the ATX power supply and unplug the power cord from the wall socket before touching components or installing the CPU.
2. Protect components from static electricity by using a grounded wrist strap or touching a safely grounded metal object, and handle components by their edges.
3. Confirm the motherboard has the surface-mount LGA1151 CPU socket and install only a CPU designed for LGA1151; do not use LGA1150, LGA1155, or LGA1156 CPUs.
4. Before installation, check that the PnP cap is on the socket and that the socket contacts are not bent. Contact the retailer immediately if the cap is missing or if the cap, socket contacts, or motherboard components are damaged.
5. Keep the PnP cap after installation because RMA service requires the motherboard to be returned with the cap on the LGA1151 socket.
6. If using the CPU Installation Tool, make sure the CPU is firmly clicked into the tool before placing it onto the socket. Use the tool only for CPU installation, do not bend or damage it, and always hold both sides of the tool when installing, removing, or picking it up.",[]

"The motherboard system memory section says this board has four 288-pin DDR4 DIMM sockets labeled DIMM_A1, DIMM_A2, DIMM_B1, and DIMM_B2. Use DDR4 memory only; DDR, DDR2, and DDR3 modules must not be installed in these DDR4 slots, and DIMM voltage below 1.65 V is recommended to protect the CPU.<PIC>\nIt supports 2 GB, 4 GB, 8 GB, and 16 GB unbuffered non-ECC DDR4 DIMMs. For best compatibility, install one DIMM in A2; install two DIMMs in the same-color A2/B2 slots for dual-channel operation; or install four DIMMs in A1/A2 and B1/B2 as two dual-channel pairs.<PIC>\nMemory sizes may differ between Channel A and Channel B, but only the lower-sized channel amount runs as dual-channel and the extra memory from the larger channel runs as single-channel. With a 32-bit operating system, 4 GB or more installed memory may leave about 3 GB or less usable, so use up to 3 GB on 32-bit or use a 64-bit operating system for 4 GB or more. For compatibility, use DIMMs with the same CAS latency, preferably the same vendor/version/date code, and use better memory cooling for four DIMMs or overclocking.",["Manual25_18", "Manual25_19"]

"The 10-1 pin COM connector is the motherboard header for a serial (COM) port. Connect the serial port module cable to this COM header, then install the module in a slot opening at the back of the system chassis; the COM module itself is purchased separately. The illustration shows the motherboard location and the COM header/pin layout for that cable connection.<PIC>",["Manual25_39"]

"The 14-1 pin TPM connector supports a Trusted Platform Module system. It is used with TPM hardware that securely stores keys, digital certificates, passwords, and data, and it helps enhance network security, protect digital identities, and ensure platform integrity. The motherboard manual labels the TPM connector as shown here.<PIC>",["Manual25_40"]

"The 2-pin T_SENSOR connector is for a thermistor cable. Connect the separately purchased thermistor cable to T_SENSOR1; the connector pins are GND and SENSOR IN.<PIC>\nIt lets the motherboard monitor the temperature of critical motherboard components and connected devices. In BIOS, the Monitor menu can display T_Sensor Temperature, and T_Sensor can be selected as the temperature source for Chassis Fan 1/2/3 or Extension Fan 1/2/3 Q-Fan Source when those Q-Fan controls are enabled.",["Manual25_43"]

"To set Quick Release, press the quick release button down until it clicks and locks in the Vent position.<PIC>\nSteam will eject through the top of the steam release valve while venting; keep hands, face, and exposed skin away from the valve.<PIC>\nWhen pressure has fully released, the float valve drops into the lid. Do not try to open the lid before the float valve has dropped.",["multi-use_pressure_cooker_and_air_fryer_01", "multi-use_pressure_cooker_and_air_fryer_02"]

"Use only the Instant Pot Duo Crisp pressure cooking lid with the Duo Crisp cooker base. To fit and close it, align the symbol on the lid with the symbol on the cooker base, lower the lid onto the track, then turn the lid clockwise until the lid symbol aligns with the cooker-base symbol.<PIC>\nBefore pressure cooking, make sure the appliance is properly closed and the sealing ring is installed; the sealing ring creates the airtight seal between the lid and the inner pot. For the quick release button, the lid says `Turn to Seal`, but the manual describes this as a flick: do not turn it more than 1/4 turn, or 45 degrees, and let it spring back to its original position.\nAfter pressure cooking, release pressure before opening the lid. Do not try to remove the lid while the float valve is up; wait until the float valve drops.",["Manual30_13"]

"To install the float valve, drop the narrow end into the float-valve hole on top of the pressure-cooking lid. Turn the lid over, then firmly attach the silicone cap to the bottom of the float valve; the cap is shown being pressed onto the valve underside.<PIC>\nDo not operate the pressure cooker unless the float valve and silicone cap are properly installed.",["Manual30_37"]

"The anti-block shield is the perforated round cover on the underside of the pressure cooking lid beside the steam release pipe. It keeps food particles from coming up through the steam release pipe and helps regulate pressure.<PIC>
Check the steam release valve, steam release pipe, anti-block shield, float valve, heating element, and air fryer lid element cover before and after use.
To remove the anti-block shield, hold the lid like a steering wheel and press firmly against the side of the shield until it pops off.<PIC>
To install it, place it over the prongs and press down until it snaps into position.<PIC>
Do not operate the pressure cooker without the anti-block shield installed.",["Manual30_19", "Manual30_34", "Manual30_35"]

"Use the quick release button on the pressure cooking lid; it controls the steam release valve. For sealing, flick the button to the Seal position, then let it spring back. The lid says Turn to Seal, but do not turn the button more than 1/4 turn, or 45 degrees. For venting or quick release, press the button down until it clicks and locks in the Vent position. The labeled positions are Seal: flick to seal, and Vent: press to vent.<PIC> When venting, steam ejects from the top of the steam release valve. Keep hands, face, and exposed skin away, do not cover or block the valve, and open the lid only after pressure is fully released and the float valve drops.",["Manual30_14"]

"To install the anti-block shield, place it over the prongs on the pressure cooking lid and press down until it snaps into position.<PIC>\nDo not operate the pressure cooker without the anti-block shield installed; it keeps food particles from entering the steam release pipe and helps regulate pressure.",["Manual30_35"]

"To install the float valve and silicone cap, drop the narrow end of the float valve into the float valve hole on top of the lid, then turn the lid over and firmly attach the silicone cap to the bottom of the float valve.<PIC> Do not operate the pressure cooker unless the float valve and silicone cap are properly installed.",["Manual30_37"]

"1. Before cooking, place the condensation collector at the back of the cooker base; it catches overflow from the condensation rim.\n2. Align the collector grooves over the tabs on the back of the cooker base, then slide the collector into place.<PIC>\n3. After each use, empty and rinse the collector.",["Manual30_38"]

"1. Place the sealing ring over the stainless-steel sealing ring rack on the underside of the pressure cooking lid.\n2. Press the ring into place all the way around the rack so it is seated behind the rack, not lying loose across the lid.<PIC>\n3. Before using the cooker, confirm only one sealing ring is installed. Use only authorised Instant Pot sealing rings, and do not use one that is stretched, deformed, or damaged.",["Manual30_31"]

"To remove the silicone sealing ring, grip the edge of the silicone and pull it out from behind the stainless-steel sealing ring rack.<PIC> Only use authorised Instant Pot sealing rings, do not use one that is stretched or damaged, and replace the ring every 12-18 months or sooner if it is stretched, deformed, or damaged.",["Manual30_30"]

"On the Instant Pot Duo Crisp, the sealing ring is used with the pressure cooking lid. When the lid is closed, it forms the airtight seal between the lid and inner pot, so it must be installed before using the cooker. Install only one sealing ring at a time, and use only authorised Instant Pot sealing rings. Do not use a stretched, deformed, or damaged sealing ring; replace it every 12-18 months, or sooner if it is stretched, deformed, or damaged. The ring sits around the underside of the pressure cooking lid behind the stainless-steel sealing ring rack.<PIC>
To remove it for cleaning or replacement, grip the edge of the silicone and pull it out from behind the stainless-steel sealing ring rack.<PIC>
To reinstall it, place the sealing ring over the sealing ring rack and press it into place.<PIC>
Clean the cooker and parts after each use. Always unplug the cooker and let it cool to room temperature before cleaning, and do not use metal scouring pads, abrasive powders, or harsh chemical detergents. Discoloration after machine washing does not affect safety or performance.",["Manual30_16", "Manual30_30", "Manual30_31"]

"The steam release valve is part of the Instant Pot Duo Crisp pressure cooking lid. It sits loosely on the steam release pipe, and when pressure is released, steam ejects from the top of the valve.<PIC>
The quick release button controls the steam release valve. For Quick Release, press the quick release button down until it clicks and locks into the Vent position; steam then vents from the top of the steam release valve, and pressure is fully released when the float valve drops into the lid.<PIC>
Do not cover or block the steam release valve, and do not place hands, face, or exposed skin over it while the appliance is operating or still has residual pressure. If steam escapes abnormally from the steam release valve, float valve, or lid sides, turn the appliance off and let it depressurise before opening.
For cleaning or maintenance, remove the valve by pulling it up and off the steam release pipe. Reinstall it by placing it on the pipe and pressing down firmly; it should still sit loosely when installed correctly.<PIC>",["Manual30_15", "multi-use_pressure_cooker_and_air_fryer_02", "Manual30_32"]

"The quick release button on the pressure cooking lid controls the steam release valve and determines whether pressure is released. It sits on the lid and has a Seal position for sealing and a Vent position for venting.<PIC>
For Quick Release, press the quick release button down until it clicks and locks into the Vent position; this stops cooking quickly and helps prevent overcooking, so use it for quick-cooking vegetables and delicate seafood.<PIC>
When the button is pressed down, steam ejects through the top of the steam release valve; after pressure has fully released, the float valve drops into the lid.<PIC>
Although the lid says Turn to Seal, the sealing motion is a flick. Do not turn the button more than 1/4 turn, or 45 degrees, and let it spring back to its original position. Keep hands, face, and exposed skin away from the steam release valve.",["Manual30_14", "multi-use_pressure_cooker_and_air_fryer_01", "multi-use_pressure_cooker_and_air_fryer_02"]

"Natural Release, also called NR or NPR, lets the Instant Pot Duo Crisp stop cooking gradually: as the temperature drops, the cooker depressurises naturally over time. Leave the quick release button in the Seal position after cooking completes and turn Keep Warm off; the food continues carryover cooking while pressure falls, and depressurisation can take up to 40 minutes depending on the food and liquid volume, type, and temperature.<PIC>\nUse Natural Release for high-starch foods such as soups, stews, chilis, pasta, oatmeal, and congee, and for expanding foods such as beans and grains. Do not open the lid until all internal pressure is released: if the float valve is still up or the lid is difficult to open, the cooker is still pressurised. When pressure is fully released, the float valve drops into the lid.",["Manual30_9"]

"The Roomba robot vacuum anatomy includes the top-side parts: iAdapt Localization Camera, handle, faceplate, RCON sensor, light touch sensor, dust bin and filter, and bin release button.<PIC>
Its Home Base charging station includes the RCON sensor, IR sensor window, and charging contacts.
The bottom-side parts include brush caps, dirt disposal port, dust bin, brush frame release tab, cliff sensors, floor tracking sensor, multi-surface brushes, edge-sweeping brush, charging contacts, and caster wheel.<PIC>
The controls and indicators are the light ring indicator, CLEAN / Power button, Dock / Home button, Spot Clean button, Wi-Fi indicator, troubleshooting indicator, battery indicator, full bin indicator, and Dirt Detect indicator.<PIC>",["Manual32_1", "Manual32_3", "Manual32_2"]

"The two modes are Virtual Wall Mode and Halo Mode. Virtual Wall Mode creates a linear barrier to block openings up to 10 ft (3 m). Halo Mode creates a circular barrier about 12 in (30 cm) from the center of the device to keep the robot away from areas such as a pet bowl, vase, or under a desk.",[]

"Empty the bin after each use.
1. Press the bin release button and remove the bin from the robot; the bin slides out from the rear of the robot.<PIC>
2. Open the bin door to empty the contents.<PIC>
3. Place the bin back in the robot.",["Manual32_8", "Manual32_9"]

"Clean the Roomba i5 filter once per week, or twice per week in homes with pets.
1. Remove the bin, then remove the filter by grasping both ends and pulling it out; the filter sits inside the bin under the bin door.<PIC>
2. Remove debris by tapping the filter against a rubbish bin.<PIC>
3. Reinsert the filter with the ridged grips facing out, then place the bin back in the robot.
Important: Do not wash the filter. Remove it before washing the bin. The robot will not run if the filter is not installed correctly, and the filter should be replaced every two months.",["Manual32_10", "Manual32_11"]

"Clean the full bin sensors once every 2 weeks.
1. Remove and empty the bin; the bin pulls out of the robot before cleaning begins.<PIC>
2. Wipe the inner full-bin sensors inside the robot with a clean, dry cloth.<PIC>
3. Wipe the bin door and its inner and outer sensor ports with a clean, dry cloth.<PIC>",["Manual32_12", "Manual32_13", "Manual32_14"]

"Clean the extractors, called Multi-Surface Brushes, this way:
1. Pinch the brush frame release tab, lift the tab, and remove any obstruction from the cleaning head.<PIC>
2. Remove both brushes from the robot. Pull the brush caps off the brush ends, clear hair or debris from under the caps, then reinstall the caps.<PIC>
3. Clear hair and debris from the square and hexagonal pegs on the opposite ends of the brushes.<PIC>
4. Remove the bin from the robot and clear debris from the vacuum path.<PIC>
5. Reinstall the brushes. Match each brush peg shape to the matching brush icon shape in the cleaning head module.",["Manual32_19", "Manual32_20", "Manual32_21", "Manual32_22"]

"Clean the sensors and charging contacts about once per month.
1. Turn the robot over and wipe the sensors with a clean, dry cloth; the bottom-view figure shows the floor tracking sensor and other sensor areas to clean.<PIC>
2. Wipe the charging contacts on both the robot and the Home Base with a clean, dry cloth; the contact figure shows both sets of charging contacts.<PIC>
3. If debris has built up on the robot or Home Base charging contacts, gently remove it with a pad of melamine foam.
4. Do not spray cleaning solution or water onto the sensors or sensor openings.",["Manual32_17", "Manual32_18"]

"Position the Home Base Charging Station in a location with good Wi-Fi coverage. Keep at least 1.5 ft (0.5 m) clear on both sides, at least 4 ft (1.2 m) clear in front, and at least 4 ft (1.2 m) away from stairs. The stair clearance is a safety requirement to help prevent the robot from falling down stairs.",[]

"Clean the front caster wheel once per month.
1. Pull firmly on the front wheel module to remove it from the robot.
2. Pull firmly on the wheel to remove it from its housing; the wheel, axle, and housing are the parts shown here.<PIC>
3. Remove debris from inside the wheel cavity.
4. Reinstall all parts and make sure the wheel clicks back into place.
Important: a front wheel clogged with hair and debris could damage the floor. If the wheel does not spin freely after cleaning, contact Customer Care.",["Manual32_15"]

"Clean the side brush once per month. The manual calls it the Edge-Sweeping Brush.
1. Use a small screwdriver to loosen the screw holding the Edge-Sweeping Brush in place.<PIC>
2. Pull the Edge-Sweeping Brush off the robot.
3. Remove any hair or debris.
4. Reinstall the brush.",["Manual32_16"]

"The Dual Mode Virtual Wall Barrier keeps the Roomba in the areas you want cleaned and out of areas you do not want it to enter. In Virtual Wall mode it creates a straight barrier that blocks openings up to 10 ft (3 m). In Halo mode it creates a circular keep-out zone around items such as a pet bowl, vase, or under a desk, reaching about 12 in (30 cm) from the device.",[]

"If the Roomba indicates a problem by playing an audio alert and turning the light ring red, press CLEAN to hear details about the issue. For more help, use the iRobot HOME App, which provides additional support and instructional videos. If you are not sure whether the robot is finished or paused, check the app for its current status.",[]

"Use the included drop-ceiling T-rail hardware; it fits most 9/16", 15/16", or 1 1/2" T-rails.
1. Use the dashed lines on the mount-plate template to set the correct spacing between the T-rail clips for the ceiling rail.<PIC>
2. Tighten the set screws on the T-rail clips, securing them with a 5/64" (2 mm) hex key at the locations shown.<PIC>
3. Fasten the mount plate to the T-rail clips through the mount-plate holes marked G.<PIC>
4. Rotate the T-rail clips onto the ceiling T-rail and snap them into place; after installation, the black foam pads should be slightly compressed.<PIC>
5. For standard mounting, use 6-32 x 4 mm T-rail screws. For a recessed T-rail, use the recessed-rail modification hardware shown: rubber spacers with 6-32 x 7 mm screws.<PIC>",["Manual33_10", "Manual33_11", "Manual33_12", "Manual33_13", "Manual33_14"]

"1. Remove the cable guard.\n2. Connect the PoE cable to the camera; if the cord exits through the top, loop the cable inside the camera as shown.<PIC>\n3. Route the Ethernet cable from an active port on an 802.3af PoE switch or PoE injector. Keep the Power over Ethernet cable run within 300 ft (100 m).",["Manual33_3"]

"Use the brake lever to stop the snowmobile by squeezing it toward the handlebar; the brake light illuminates when the brake is operated. When slowing or stopping, release the throttle and apply the brake gently, not suddenly.\nTo set the parking brake with the brake button, squeeze the brake lever, push down the parking brake button while releasing the lever, and leave it held in place. To release the parking brake, squeeze the brake lever again. Always set the parking brake before starting the engine, but never run the machine with the parking brake applied because it can overheat the brake disc and reduce braking ability.",[]

"Before each use, perform the pre-operation checks before starting the engine.
1. Make sure there is enough fuel and engine oil; use unleaded gasoline rated 88 pump octane or higher and YAMALUBE 2-cycle oil.
2. Check that the throttle lever opens smoothly and springs back to idle, the manual starter works, and the starter rope is not damaged.
3. Check the Throttle Override System with the parking brake applied; if the engine does not stop during the check, turn the main switch OFF and consult a dealer.
4. Test the brake at slow speed when starting out. Do not operate the machine if braking is not proper.
5. Inspect the V-belt for wear or damage, and make sure the V-belt guard mounts, wing bolt, and guard are secure; never run the engine without the V-belt or with the guard removed.
6. Check the drive track for deflection, wear, damage, and misadjustment. Do not operate with track damage or misadjustment because braking ability and control could be lost.
7. Check slide runners, skis, and ski runners for wear or damage, and replace them when they reach their wear limits.
8. Check steering for excessive free play, check the lights, battery fluid level, and tightness of fittings and fasteners.
9. Carry service tools and spare parts; for a long-distance trip, also carry extra fuel and oil.",[]

"Before starting, review the safety information, perform the pre-operation checks, confirm the throttle, steering, and brake work properly, apply the parking brake, make sure no one is standing behind the machine, and pull the engine stop switch to the ON position.
1. For a cold engine, fully open the starter lever (choke). For a warm engine, do not use the choke; leave the starter lever in the Close position.
2. On a manual-start model, turn the main switch to ON, pull the recoil starter slowly until it engages, then pull it briskly.<PIC>
3. On an electric-start model, turn the main switch to START. Release the switch immediately after the engine starts; if it does not start, release the switch, wait a few seconds, and try again. Keep each attempt short and do not crank for more than 10 seconds at a time.<PIC>
4. After the engine starts, warm it up. On electric-start models, first put the starter lever in the Half-open position, then continue warming the engine until it runs smoothly and does not stall when the starter lever is returned to Close.
5. Do not run the machine with the parking brake applied after starting, because this can overheat the brake disc and reduce braking ability.",["Manual34_116", "Manual34_117"]

"A V-belt holder is the provided storage holder for keeping a spare snowmobile V-belt for emergency use. Loosen the bolt to remove the belt, tighten the bolt when installing the belt in the holder, make sure the fuel hose is not pinched and is in place, and install the belt securely so it cannot come loose and be damaged by the hot muffler.",[]

"Adjust the throttle cable only after the engine idle speed has been adjusted first.
1. Loosen the adjuster locknut.
2. Turn the adjuster in or out until the throttle lever free play is correct: BR250T/ET410TR/VK540E 1.0 to 2.0 mm (0.04 to 0.08 in), or CS340E 0.5 to 1.0 mm (0.02 to 0.04 in).<PIC>
3. Tighten the locknut.
4. Before riding, confirm that the throttle lever opens smoothly and springs back to idle when released.",["Manual34_148"]

"Before riding, check the steering system for excessive free play: push the handlebar up and down and back and forth, then turn it slightly right and left. If there is excessive free play, have a dealer inspect it.\nWhen riding, keep both hands on the handlebars and both feet on the running boards. Practice steering and turning at slow speed in a large, flat, obstacle-free area before using higher speeds or difficult terrain.\nFor most snow surfaces, slow down before a curve, turn the handlebars in the direction you want to go, put your weight on the running board on the inside of the turn, and lean your upper body into the turn. Lean more for sharper or faster turns. Avoid abrupt throttle changes, excessive braking, incorrect body movement, or too much speed for the turn. If the snowmobile starts to tip, lean farther into the turn; if needed, gradually ease off the throttle and/or steer toward the outside of the turn.",[]

"1. Approach the curve at reduced speed.
2. Begin turning the handlebars in the direction you want to go.
3. At the same time, put your weight on the running board on the inside of the turn and lean your upper body into the turn.
4. Practice this at slow speed many times in a large, flat, obstacle-free area before using higher speeds or tighter curves.
5. Lean farther as the turn becomes sharper or faster.
6. Avoid abrupt throttle changes, excessive braking, incorrect body movement, and too much speed for the turn, because these can make the snowmobile tip.
7. If the snowmobile starts to tip while turning, lean more into the turn to regain balance; if needed, gradually ease off the throttle and/or steer toward the outside of the turn.",[]

"1. Practice first on gentle slopes, and attempt more difficult climbs only after you have developed the skill.
2. As you approach the hill, accelerate before starting the climb, then reduce the throttle opening to prevent track slippage.
3. Keep your weight on the uphill side at all times. For a straight uphill climb, lean forward; on steeper inclines, stand on the running boards and lean forward over the handlebars.
4. Slow down as you reach the crest, and be ready for obstacles, sharp drops, or other people or vehicles on the other side.
5. If you cannot continue uphill, do not spin the track. Stop the engine, set the parking brake, pull the rear of the snowmobile around until it points downhill, and do not get on the downhill side of the machine. Then restart the engine, release the parking brake, and descend the hill.
Side hills and steep slopes are not recommended for a beginner or novice snowmobiler.",[]

"1. Keep downhill speed to a minimum.
2. Use just enough throttle to keep the clutch engaged while descending, so engine compression helps slow the snowmobile and prevents it from rolling freely downhill. The downhill-riding illustration shows the controlled descent this section describes.<PIC>
3. Apply the brake frequently, but use only light pressure.
4. Use extra caution with braking during descent; excessive braking can lock the track and cause loss of control.",["Manual34_128"]

"Side hills are not recommended for beginner or novice snowmobilers. When crossing the face of a slope, keep the snowmobile balanced by shifting your weight toward the uphill side. A recommended position is to kneel with the downhill-leg knee on the seat and keep the uphill-leg foot on the running board so you can shift body weight as needed.<PIC>\nIf the snowmobile begins to slip sideways and the path is clear of obstacles, steer in the direction of the slide. As balance returns, gradually steer back toward the direction you want to travel. If the snowmobile starts to tip, steer down the hill to regain balance. If you cannot maintain balance and it is going to tip over, dismount immediately on the uphill side. On ice or icy surfaces, drive slowly and cautiously because traction for turning, stopping, and starting is much lower; avoid rapid acceleration, turning, or braking.",["Manual34_129"]

"1. Periodically remove the spark plug and inspect the white porcelain insulator around the center electrode. A normally used machine should show a medium to light tan color.
2. If the plug color is distinctly different, do not try to diagnose the engine yourself. A very white center-electrode porcelain can indicate an intake-tract air leak or carburetion problem, so take the machine to a dealer.
3. Use only a spark plug with the proper reach: BR250T is 12.7 mm (0.50 in), and CS340E/ET410TR/VK540E is 19.0 mm (0.75 in).<PIC>
4. Before installing the plug, measure the electrode gap with a wire thickness gauge and adjust it to specification; the plug drawing identifies reach as 1 and electrode gap as 2.<PIC>
5. Clean the gasket surface, wipe grime from the threads, and torque the spark plug to 28 Nm (2.8 m-kg, 20 ft-lb). Consult your dealer before changing to a different type of spark plug.",["Manual34_138", "Manual34_139"]

"To clean the snowmobile for storage:
1. Thoroughly clean the machine inside and out to remove corrosive salts and acids.
2. Use Mud and Grease Release, or an equivalent product, to loosen mud, grease, and grime.
3. Wash with mild soap.
4. Rinse the machine.
5. Dry it completely.
6. After cleaning, apply wax such as Silicone Wax to painted surfaces, then spray Silicone Spray or an equivalent protectant on the exterior of the engine, track, and other metal, plastic, and rubber parts.",[]

"For TV channel memorizing by MANUAL PROGRAM, use either method:\n1. With the remote control, press the up/down or number buttons to choose the channel number you want to memorize or erase, then press MEMORY/ERASE to choose Memory or Erase.\n2. Through the menu, press MENU and use up/down until the channel menu appears. Press right and choose Manual, then choose Channel. Select the channel number, choose Memory, set it to On to memorize the channel or Off to erase it, then repeatedly press MENU to exit.",[]

"Common causes and conditions include electrical interference from automobile ignition systems, neon lamps, electrical drills, and other electrical appliances; ghosting when the TV signal reaches the receiver by both a direct path and a reflected path from tall buildings, hills, or other objects; weak fringe-area TV signals that create small dots or snow; old, bad, or illegally recorded tapes; strong random signals from cars or airplanes; weak antenna signals; and severely weathered antennas or antenna cables that reduce signal quality.",[]

"1. To set the on-screen display language, press MENU, use the up/down button until the Special menu is shown, choose Language with the right button and up/down buttons, then choose the language with the right and up/down buttons; from then on, the on-screen display appears in that language. The Special menu contains the Language and Caption entries used for these settings.<PIC>
2. To turn captions or text on with the remote shortcut, press CAPTION, press CAPTION again until the setting you want appears, then press ENTER (OK) to exit. The shortcut cycles through Off, Mode1, Mode2, Text1, and Text2, with the current caption mode shown on screen.<PIC>
3. To change the same setting through the menu, press MENU, use up/down to select the Special menu, press the right button and use up/down to select Caption, then press the right button and use up/down to choose Off, Mode1, Mode2, Text1, or Text2; repeatedly press MENU to exit. The Caption menu shows the same mode choices and highlights the selected one.<PIC>
4. Use Mode1 for the usual caption signal; Mode2 may show demonstration or programming information. Text1/Text2 use full-screen text services such as program lists, weather, stock, or news when the station provides them. Not all broadcasts include caption or text signals, poor reception can disrupt them, and a black box may appear in Text mode during poor signal conditions. The TV remembers the last caption/text mode after power-off.",["Manual35_4", "Manual35_37", "Manual35_38"]

"1. Use an outdoor antenna for best reception, and inspect the antenna and antenna cable first; severely weathered parts can reduce signal quality.\n2. Identify the cable type. If it is 300 ohm flat wire, attach the wire to the screws on a 300 ohm to 75 ohm adapter, then push the adapter into the TV's 75 ohm antenna jack. If it is 75 ohm coaxial cable, connect the coaxial cable directly to the 75 ohm antenna jack. The diagram shows both correct paths into the TV rear 75 ohm antenna jack: 300 ohm flat wire through the 300-to-75 ohm adapter, or 75 ohm coaxial cable directly.<PIC>\n3. After connecting, aim for reception quality. If you see ghosts, change the direction or position of the antenna. If the TV is in a weak fringe signal area and the picture has small dots, use a special antenna to improve the picture.",["Manual35_39"]

"1. Connect the DVD player's component video outputs to the TV's component DVD input jacks, matching Y to Y, PB to PB, and PR to PR for 480i component input. Then connect the DVD player's right and left audio outputs to the TV's component AUDIO R/L inputs.<PIC>
2. If the DVD player uses alternate component labels, match Y to Y, Cb/B-Y/Pb/PB to PB, and Cr/R-Y/Pr/PR to PR.<PIC>
3. Turn on the TV and the DVD player, then press TV/VIDEO on the remote control or the TV front panel until COMPONENT is selected.
4. For an audio amplifier or other audio system used for monitoring the selected program from the TV, connect the audio system's inputs to the TV's OUT A/V jacks instead of the IN jacks.",["Manual35_43", "Manual35_44"]

"Use the troubleshooting checklist by matching the symptom to the marked checks. For no power, no picture or sound, poor picture, poor reception, lines, ghosts, remote-control failure, or inability to tune a channel, check whether the power cord is plugged in, whether the television is turned on, the remote-control batteries, another channel to rule out station trouble, the antenna connections, local interference, antenna position, picture control settings, and whether the channel has been programmed with AUTO PROGRAM.<PIC>\nIf the checklist does not resolve the problem or the TV needs service, do not remove the cover or back; refer servicing to qualified personnel.",["Manual35_45"]

"1. Keep the television away from rain, moisture, dripping, splashing, and objects filled with liquids.
2. Do not remove the cover or back, and do not try to service the television yourself; there are no user-serviceable parts inside, so servicing should be done by qualified personnel.
3. Operate the television only from the power source shown on the marking label, protect the power cord from being walked on or pinched, and do not overload wall outlets or extension cords.
4. Use a stable cart, stand, table, shelf, or wall mount that follows the manufacturer's instructions, and keep all ventilation openings unblocked to prevent overheating.
5. Unplug the television before cleaning, use only a damp cloth, avoid liquid or aerosol cleaners, and do not use attachments not recommended by the manufacturer.
6. Never push objects through cabinet slots or spill liquid into the television. Before operation, make sure the antenna or cable connection is complete and the set is plugged into a power outlet.",[]

"Choose one of the three intensity levels: High uses three lights, Medium uses two lights, and Low uses one light.<PIC>
1. To change the intensity from the handle, press the intensity indicator lights/hidden button to cycle through the levels.
2. You can change the intensity before, during, or after brushing.<PIC>
3. You can also customize the intensity in the app.
4. If Adaptive Intensity is active and you apply excess pressure for an extended period, the toothbrush automatically lowers the intensity by 1 level; each new brushing cycle returns to your pre-selected setting.",["toothbrush0_06", "toothbrush0_07"]

"Use the app for Adaptive Intensity and Scrubbing Feedback, and use the handle for Adaptive Intensity, Pressure Sensor Feedback, and the brush head replacement reminder. To customize brushing intensity, press the intensity indicator lights on the handle to cycle through High, Medium, and Low; this can be done before, during, or after brushing and can also be set in the app.
1. For app-based changes, download and open the app, turn on phone Bluetooth, pick up the toothbrush so its lights are on, follow the guided pairing steps, create an account, update firmware if prompted, then enable or disable Adaptive Intensity and Scrubbing Feedback in the app.
2. For handle-based changes, place the handle on the charging stand, then press and hold the power button for the feature you want to change; this is the handle control used for feature activation or deactivation.<PIC>
3. Hold up to 7 seconds for Adaptive Intensity, up to 3 seconds for Pressure Sensor Feedback, or up to 5 seconds for the brush head replacement reminder.
4. Confirm the result at the battery indicator: white blinking 3 times with 3 tones from low to high means the feature is activated; amber blinking 3 times with 3 tones from high to low means it is deactivated.<PIC>
5. Adaptive Intensity is deactivated when Pressure Sensor Feedback is deactivated. Defaults are: Adaptive Intensity on, Pressure Sensor Feedback on, Scrubbing Feedback off, and brush head replacement reminder on.",["Manual37_13", "toothbrush0_11"]

"1. Plug the USB cord into the travel case, then plug the other end into the USB wall adapter.<PIC>
2. Plug the wall adapter into an electrical outlet.
3. Place the toothbrush handle into the travel case.<PIC>
4. Check that charging has started: the toothbrush should beep twice, the lights should illuminate in an upward motion, and the battery indicator should blink white while charging.<PIC>
5. Leave the travel case plugged in until the toothbrush is fully charged; the battery light turns off when charging is complete. For stability, place the travel case on its side.<PIC>",["Manual37_18", "Manual37_19", "Manual37_20", "Manual37_21"]
